source_input.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

pinterest_cluster_labels: Neutral Japandi living with low coffee table

pins.title: Japandi Dining Room with Neutral Decor
pins.description: A Japandi dining room with neutral décor blends Japanese simplicity and Scandinavian warmth. Soft beige, warm wood, and gentle earth tones create a calm, inviting space. Clean lines, natural materials, and minimal furnishings keep the room feeling open and uncluttered, while subtle textures like linen, ceramic, and light wood add quiet elegance and comfort.

pins.title: Japandi Living Room: Calm Minimal Decor, Natural Wood & Indoor Plants
pins.description: Calm Japandi living room inspiration with a light neutral sofa, low wooden coffee table, soft textured rug, and plenty of indoor plants for a serene, balanced space. Minimal wall art, natural materials, and clean lines create an airy, soothing aesthetic—perfect for Japanese style interiors, minimalist living room ideas, and warm neutral home decor. Save for Japandi decor and plant-filled living room styling.
pins.cluster_label: Neutral Japandi living with low coffee table

cluster_summary.label: Japandi dining with rattan pendant
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Round oak dining table, woven rattan pendant, light linen-upholstered chairs, ceramic tableware. Stage with a single textured centerpiece (stoneware vase), neutral place settings, and warm-toned pendant to create an intimate focal point. Keep surfaces decluttered and emphasize wood grain and woven textures.

cluster_summary.label: Low-seating Japanese tea nook
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Floor-level cushions and low table on layered natural-fibre rug, bamboo/blind screens and soft lantern lighting. Arrange poufs and floor cushions for casual seating, add braided straw floor pads and low ambient lamps to enhance cozy, contemplative mood. Use warm, dimmable light and sparse greenery for balance.

cluster_summary.label: Neutral Japandi living with low coffee table
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Light neutral sofa on low wooden base, oversized low-profile coffee table, potted plants and minimal wall art. Stage with textured rugs (wool + tatami edge), mixed neutral cushions, sculptural ceramics and small bonsai or leafy plants to introduce organic color and scale. Emphasize horizontal lines and negative space.

vibe_report.summary: A calm, warm Japandi palette that blends Japanese minimalism with Scandinavian warmth. Prioritize natural materials, low-profile furniture, layered textiles, and restrained accessories. Create a cozy family-friendly environment by integrating a crib in the bedroom and a compact standing desk in a quiet living corner. Keep compositions breathable with negative space and a limited, warm-neutral palette.
vibe_report.palette_rationale: Warm neutrals (creams, soft beiges, honeyed oak, muted browns and charcoal accents) support a meditative and family-friendly atmosphere. Use lighter neutrals on major planes (walls, large textiles) and mid-tone wood and ceramic accents to anchor furniture and focal objects. Reserve the darkest tones for small architectural trim, fixtures, and minimal art to add depth without visual weight.
vibe_report.lighting_mood: Soft ambient daylight with controlled directional pulls from visible windows/doors; selectively add warm practicals (floor lamp, pendant) for evening scenes. Maintain believable light-source logic: sunlight from exterior openings, warm practical fixtures near seating and the desk, and diffused skylike fill where needed.
furniture_language: clean, simple silhouettes with subtle joinery
rounded edges on tables and pendants
minimal shelving and closed storage to reduce visual clutter
compact standing desk with cable management
styling_rules: Limit palette to 4–5 warm neutrals; avoid saturated hues.
Emphasize horizontal lines and low furniture scale.
One sculptural ceramic or stone centerpiece per table; keep surfaces decluttered.
Use layered rugs to define low seating/tea nook and living zones.
Include a small, sparse set of plants (bonsai, leafy pot) for organic color.
avoid: tall, heavy furniture that breaks horizontal calm
introducing light sources that contradict window direction

room_guidance.headline: Calm, multipurpose living room with a cozy work corner
room_guidance.guidance: Lay out a low-profile sofa on a neutral rug facing the wide glass door to the patio. Add a low wooden coffee table, a rattan pendant or floor lamp for warm practical light, and a compact standing desk tucked behind/adjacent to the sofa—preferably near an interior window for task light. Keep one wall minimal with a single low shelf or piece of art. Maintain clear circulation to patio doors.
must_include: rattan pendant or warm floor lamp
must_avoid: bright patterns or saturated accent pillows
placing the desk directly in the main traffic path

room_guidance.headline: Serene bedroom with family-ready crib and calming textiles
room_guidance.guidance: Center a low wood platform bed against the primary wall; place a compact crib near the visible window area for natural light and caregiver sightlines. Use layered neutral bedding, a soft wool rug, and minimal bedside pendants or sconces. A small corner bench or built-in shelf for baby essentials keeps surfaces tidy. Prioritize safe clearances around crib and bed.
must_avoid: overhead bright fixtures directly above crib
crowding crib with large toys or heavy textiles

room_guidance.headline: Intimate Japandi dining nook with rattan pendant
room_guidance.guidance: Use a round oak table with 3–4 light linen-upholstered chairs and a woven rattan pendant centered above. Keep a single stoneware vase as a centerpiece. Place a soft, low-pile natural rug under the table if floor material allows. Ensure window panels are staged to show exterior brightness but not overexposed.
must_avoid: heavy dining table runners or visual clutter on table

room_guidance.headline: Simple, functional kitchen sightlines with warm materials
room_guidance.guidance: Keep counters clear; show natural wood cutting boards, matte ceramic canisters, and one small plant. If photographing, use the adjacent dining light and window as main fills—do not invent new directional lighting. Emphasize hardware with matte finishes and consistent warm tones.
must_include: soft natural task light from dining area

room_guidance.headline: Covered patio as an extension of living: low seating and lanterns
room_guidance.guidance: Stage low-seating (cushioned bench or floor cushions), a small teak side table, and a couple of woven lanterns for warm evening ambience. Keep outdoor textiles durable and neutral. Compose shots to show strong opening between interior and patio for continuity.

warnings: Preserve all measured architecture and circulation—do not propose furniture that blocks doorways or patio access.
All lighting and shadow direction must originate from visible windows/doors or practical fixtures; avoid introducing unseen light sources.
Crib placement must follow basic safety spacing—do not stage heavy hanging objects directly above.

creative_spec.overall_mood: Calm, meditative warmth — family-ready Japandi with breathable layouts and tactile materials.
global_style_notes: Preserve horizontal, low-profile sightlines and negative space across scenes.
Keep color temperature warm and neutral; avoid cool blues and saturated primaries.
Maintain visible light-source logic in every shot (sunlight from doors/windows and warm practical fixtures).
Favor single-object focal points (stoneware vase, bonsai, pendant) rather than clusters.
creative_spec.negative_prompt: blur, distort, low quality, warped architecture, flicker, unstable camera, unrealistic lighting, muddy shadows, layout drift, impossible light sources

caption_context.property_brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.
caption_context.aesthetic_summary: A calm, warm Japandi palette that blends Japanese minimalism with Scandinavian warmth. Prioritize natural materials, low-profile furniture, layered textiles, and restrained accessories. Create a cozy family-friendly environment by integrating a crib in the bedroom and a compact standing desk in a quiet living corner. Keep compositions breathable with negative space and a limited, warm-neutral palette.

annotations.label: Mood
annotations.value: Calm, meditative warmth — family-ready Japandi with breathable layouts and tactile materials.

warnings.message: All cinematic passes must preserve measured room geometry and verticals; reject any render with warped walls/windows.
Image-to-video workflow default: raw-photo -> image pass -> video. Run 1 editorial image pass for rooms lacking strong directional light or composition before generating motion.
Evaluation criteria: discard clips with layout drift, pulsing shadows, or inconsistent light-source logic.
Preserve all measured architecture and circulation—do not propose furniture that blocks doorways or patio access.
All lighting and shadow direction must originate from visible windows/doors or practical fixtures; avoid introducing unseen light sources.
Crib placement must follow basic safety spacing—do not stage heavy hanging objects directly above.
